findings.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
findings.what: 797 of 797 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
findings.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

findings.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
findings.what: 6982 of 6982 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
findings.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.

findings.effort: A small file for most flat-fare systems.
findings.fix: Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
findings.what: The feed contains no fare information.
findings.why: Riders see 'fare unknown' in trip planners and can't budget their trip; visitors are most affected.

findings.what: feed_info.txt has no technical contact (feed_contact_email or feed_contact_url).
findings.why: App makers and state data programs have nobody to email when they spot a problem with your feed, so problems linger.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 0% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (0% marked accessible, 0% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is not published.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Trip distance exceeds shape distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'missing_timepoint_value' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Missing timepoint value (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stop_too_far_from_shape_using_user_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stop too far from shape using user distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A few minutes per flagged trip.
findings.fix: Check the flagged stop times for transposed minutes.
findings.what: Some scheduled trips travel implausibly fast between stops.
findings.why: Usually a typo'd stop time; riders get impossible arrival estimates.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance_below_threshold' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Trip distance exceeds shape distance below threshold (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Remove past service periods the next time you export the feed.
findings.what: Some service calendars in the feed have already expired.
findings.why: Expired calendars are dead weight and can hide real schedule problems from your staff and vendors.

findings.fix: Enable 'remove unused shapes' (or similar) in your export tool.
findings.what: The feed contains route shapes no trip uses.
findings.why: Harmless to riders, but it bloats the feed and suggests stale export data.

findings.effort: One small file, set once in export settings.
findings.fix: Add feed_info.txt with publisher name, URL, language, and feed_start_date/feed_end_date.
findings.what: A recommended GTFS file (usually feed_info.txt) is missing.
findings.why: feed_info.txt tells apps who publishes the feed and when it expires; without it nobody is warned before data goes stale.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stops_match_shape_out_of_order' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stops match shape out of order (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Trim past service periods the next time you export.
findings.what: Many of the feed's trips don't run at all in the next 7 days.
findings.why: It usually means old service periods are still in the export, making the feed bigger and harder to check.

findings.effort: A quick look at the flagged files.
findings.fix: Check the flagged column names for misspellings of standard GTFS fields; remove them if they are vendor extras.
findings.what: Some files contain columns that are not part of the GTFS spec.
findings.why: Harmless to riders, but apps ignore these columns and they can hide typos in real column names.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 11 kinds of issue across 154956 instances (102 error, 154853 warning, 1 informational).

findings.effort: Usually one export setting; export on a schedule so it never lapses again.
findings.fix: Re-export the feed with a calendar that reaches further out, and set feed_info feed_end_date past your next service change.
findings.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency even though the buses are still running. Riders are told the service does not exist.

findings.effort: Two fields, set once in export settings.
findings.fix: Add feed_info.txt with feed_start_date and feed_end_date to your export.
findings.what: feed_info.txt is missing its start/end dates (the file itself is absent)
findings.why: Apps and this scorecard can't warn anyone before the feed goes stale without stated validity dates.

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data ended 182 day(s) ago. Trip planners have likely already dropped this feed.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Not scored yet. Nothing here counts against the grade.

criteria.detail: States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

conformance.summary: This feed is close to the conformance mark. 1 validator error to resolve. Service data expired 181 days ago. States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

ntd_id_alignment.detail: Setting your GTFS agency_id to your five-digit NTD ID is an optional way to line a feed up with its National Transit Database record. FTA links the two on your P-50 form, so it is not a required feed change. We don't have your NTD ID on file, so this is not checked yet.

pillars.detail: Service data expired 181 days ago, so FTA would find the link out of date.

ntd_readiness.summary: Resolve this before you certify on the D-10. 1 validator error to resolve. Service data expired 181 days ago, so FTA would find the link out of date.

recommendations.effort: A per-route color setting in your scheduling software.
recommendations.fix: Adjust route_color or route_text_color so each pair clears 4.5:1. Often switching the text between black and white is enough.
recommendations.what: 12 route badge(s) pair a color and text color below the WCAG 4.5:1 contrast bar (21, 03, 02, 05, 04, and more).
recommendations.why: Low-contrast route badges are hard to read for riders with low vision and wash out on a phone screen in sunlight at the stop.

recommendations.effort: A short text field per affected stop; start with the busiest.
recommendations.fix: Add tts_stop_name with the spoken form, e.g. 'Main Street and Second Avenue', for the affected stops.
recommendations.what: 747 stop name(s) use abbreviations or symbols a screen reader may mispronounce, with no spoken form set ("Dallas Aquatic Ctr - Lacreole @ Barberry", "Center @ Cottage", "Wallace @ Hope", and more).
recommendations.why: Riders who use a screen reader hear the raw name, so 'Main St & 2nd Ave' can come out as 'Main saint' or drop the ampersand.

top_fixes.effort: Usually one export setting; export on a schedule so it never lapses again.
top_fixes.fix: Re-export the feed with a calendar that reaches further out, and set feed_info feed_end_date past your next service change.
top_fixes.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency even though the buses are still running. Riders are told the service does not exist.

top_fixes.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
top_fixes.what: Trip distance exceeds shape distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
top_fixes.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

top_fixes.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
top_fixes.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
top_fixes.what: 797 of 797 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
top_fixes.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.
